rationale: The citing paper references Lee and Labroo (2004) in a grouped citation to illustrate the well-known background claim that processing fluency tends to increase liking. It is used as one example among several classic fluency studies, not as a focal source for this paper's hypotheses, mechanism tests, or design choices. The core theoretical development and mediation logic in the citing paper are built around disfluency as a competence cue (especially via Song and Schwarz and related work), not specifically around Lee and Labroo's conceptual/perceptual fluency framework.
